data.title: As a DSO plugin developer, I can set, get, and inspect a ticket's file impact via the CLI
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Add `ticket set-file-impact <id> <json>` to store structured file impact as a FILE_IMPACT event. Add `ticket get-file-impact <id>` to return the structured array from compiled state. Extend `ticket show <id>` output to include the file_impact field.

**Why**: Provides the user-facing CLI surface that lets consumers (and the Jira bridge) read and write file impact as structured data instead of parsing markdown.

**Scope**:
- IN: ticket-set-file-impact.sh/py, ticket-get-file-impact.sh/py, ticket show extension to include file_impact field, dispatcher integration, integration test, unit tests
- OUT: Jira bridge (Stories C, D); data migration (Story E); consumer refactors (Story F); FILE_IMPACT event type (Story 2985-f04d — blocker)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, `.claude/scripts/dso ticket set-file-impact <id> <json>` writes a FILE_IMPACT event with the supplied structured payload ← Satisfies: "ticket set-file-impact <id> <json> stores structured file impact data as a FILE_IMPACT event"
- When this story is complete, `.claude/scripts/dso ticket get-file-impact <id>` returns the structured array of {path, reason} objects from compiled state ← Satisfies: "ticket get-file-impact <id> returns the structured array"
- When this story is complete, `.claude/scripts/dso ticket show <id>` output includes the file_impact field ← Satisfies: "ticket show <id> includes the file_impact field in its output"
- When this story is complete, an integration test exercises all three surfaces through the CLI dispatcher against a synthetic fixture
- When this story is complete, unit tests pass for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] Follow existing ticket-*.sh dispatcher pattern for consistency
- [Testing] Integration test should round-trip: set-file-impact → get-file-impact → show, asserting field equivalence at each step
- [Reliability] set-file-impact must validate the input JSON matches the array-of-{path, reason} schema before writing the event

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
